name: Ruta Llobregat entre Paseo de San Vicente y puente peatonal de Castelbisbal - circular
region_intro: Mountain biking around El Papiol offers diverse landscapes and extensive trail networks within the Serra de Collserola Natural Park, just 15 km from Barcelona. The region features varied terrain, from river paths along the Llobregat to elevated areas providing panoramic views. Trails range from easy, family-friendly options to challenging routes with significant elevation changes, catering to different skill levels. This area is characterized by its blend of natural parkland, historical sites, and unique geological features like Les Escletxes. Best mountain bike trails around El Papiol The most popular mountain bike trail is Ruta Llobregat entre Paseo de San Vicente y puente peatonal de Castelbisbal - circular, a 14.3 miles (23.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 49 minutes to complete. This easy route follows the Llobregat river, offering a flat and accessible ride. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is View from the Pallejá Antenna – Pond of the Carinyo Spring loop from Pallejà, a moderate 17.7 miles (28.5 km) path. This route includes varied terrain and offers views from the Pallejá Antenna. Local mountain bikers also love the Fossada Tower – Fossada Tower (Castellbisbal) loop from Pallejà, a 14.4 miles (23.1 km) trail leading through the local landscape, often completed in about 1 hour 58 minutes. Mountain biking around El Papiol is defined by its extensive network within Parc de Collserola, varied terrain, and panoramic viewpoints. The network offers options for different ability levels, from beginner-friendly paths to more demanding climbs. The routes in El Papiol are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 from over 1200 reviews. More than 15000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore El Papiol's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around El Papiol?
A: There are over 300 mountain bike trails around El Papiol, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels. This includes 34 easy routes, 164 moderate routes, and 107 difficult routes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in El Papiol?
A: The terrain around El Papiol is highly diverse, ranging from flat river paths along the Llobregat to elevated areas within the Serra de Collserola Natural Park. You'll find a mix of thick, green, and sometimes wet trails, reminiscent of regions with lush forests, alongside more challenging, rocky sections. Some trails, like the 'Forapistas,' are known for being easy, while others, such as the 'Papiol Rock Garden extreme,' offer demanding downhill experiences.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in El Papiol?
A: Yes, El Papiol offers several family-friendly mountain bike trails. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952549708/ruta-llobregat-entre-paseo-de-san-vicente-y-puente-peatonal-de-castelbisbal-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta Llobregat entre Paseo de San Vicente y puente peatonal de Castelbisbal - circular&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy, circular route following the Llobregat river, ideal for a relaxed ride with children.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails in El Papiol?
A: Many trails within the Serra de Collserola Natural Park, which surrounds El Papiol, are suitable for riding with dogs, provided they are well-behaved and kept under control. Always check specific trail regulations within the park, but generally, the natural environment is welcoming to pets.

Q: What interesting landmarks or natural features can I see while mountain biking near El Papiol?
A: Mountain biking around El Papiol offers access to several unique landmarks and natural features. You can explore geological wonders like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/951471" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Les Escletxes&lt;/a&gt; (large rock cracks) and the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1066148" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mines de quars i Florita&lt;/a&gt;. Historical sites such as the Romanesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1059094" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of La Salut&lt;/a&gt; and various castles like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3037794" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castellciuró&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2687103" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fossada Tower (Castellbisbal)&lt;/a&gt; are also accessible along or near the trails.

Q: Are there any routes with good viewpoints in the El Papiol area?
A: Yes, El Papiol is situated on a mountain platform, offering panoramic views. Many routes within the Serra de Collserola Natural Park provide excellent vistas. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10586476" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View from the Pallejá Antenna – Pond of the Carinyo Spring loop from Pallejà&lt;/a&gt; route includes views from the Pallejá Antenna, showcasing the surrounding region.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in El Papiol?
A: El Papiol's Mediterranean climate generally allows for mountain biking year-round. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and lush scenery, making them ideal. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended. Winters are mild, though some higher elevation trails might be cooler.

Q: How can I access the mountain bike trails in El Papiol using public transport?
A: El Papiol is conveniently located just 15 km from Barcelona, and the region benefits from a bike-friendly train system. This makes it easy to reach various trailheads within the Collserola mountains from Barcelona or other nearby towns, often eliminating the need for circular routes if you plan to use public transport for one leg of your journey.

Q: Are there any permits required for mountain biking in the Serra de Collserola Natural Park?
A: While the Serra de Collserola Natural Park offers over 250 km of paths where biking is officially permitted, it's always advisable to check the latest regulations from the park authority regarding specific zones or any potential permit requirements for certain activities or areas. Generally, access to marked trails for mountain biking is open.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in El Papiol?
A: The mountain bike trails in El Papiol are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.4 out of 5 stars from over 1200 reviews. Riders frequently praise the diverse landscapes, the extensive network of trails within Parc de Collserola, and the variety of options catering to different ability levels.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking a challenge, El Papiol offers difficult routes with significant elevation changes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33904065" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorg de la Mola – La Creu d'Aragall loop from Pallejà&lt;/a&gt;, which features substantial climbs and descents, providing a demanding experience. The 'Papiol Rock Garden extreme' is also noted for its challenging downhill sections.

Q: Are there circular mountain bike routes available in El Papiol?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes around El Papiol are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. A popular easy circular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e952549708/ruta-llobregat-entre-paseo-de-san-vicente-y-puente-peatonal-de-castelbisbal-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta Llobregat entre Paseo de San Vicente y puente peatonal de Castelbisbal - circular&lt;/a&gt;. For a moderate challenge, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/34054213" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fossada Tower – Fossada Tower (Castellbisbal) loop from Pallejà&lt;/a&gt;.
